title: 2 Terminology
body_text:
2	Terminology
2.1	Definitions
Business Day
A business day is a weekday including Monday to Friday, except for statutory holidays that fall on or are observed on a weekday, including Quebec holidays.
Branch Number
The branch number is a 5-digit number used to identify the branch of a Canadian financial institution. The branch number and the institution number make up the transit number.
Client Centre
The Client Centre is a digital portal that provides secured access to Central 1芒聙聶s applications and to information and news about our products and services.
Client Centre User
Client Centre Users log in to the Client Centre to access certain applications. Client Centre Users can芒聙聶t manage Security Officers (User Management / CPS Admin Security Officers) or other Client Centre Users.
CPS Admin
The Central Portal System (CPS) Admin application is used to add, edit, and delete Client Centre Users and Security Officers.
User Management Security Officer
User Management Security Officers use the User Management or CPS Admin application to add, edit, and delete Client Centre Users and to modify and delete other User Management Security Officers (in the User Management application). User Management Security Officers are also referred to as CPS Admin Security Officers. Security Officers use the Client Centre to log in to certain applications.
Institution Number
The institution number is the 3-digit number assigned by the CPA that identifies the financial institution (e.g., 809 is for BC credit unions and 828 is for Ontario credit unions). The branch number and the institution number make up the transit number.
Major Currency
A major currency is a foreign currency that is frequently bought and sold.
Minor Currency
A minor currency is a foreign currency that isn芒聙聶t a major currency.
FX Notes Auditor
Auditors can view or download exchange rates on FX Notes and view the reports for all your financial institution芒聙聶s branches. Auditors can芒聙聶t process transactions on FX Notes.
FX Notes Manager
Managers can process foreign currency orders on FX Notes, view the reports, and manage foreign exchange spreads.
FX Notes Standard User
Standard users can process foreign currency orders on FX Notes, view exchange rates, and view orders related to the branches that they can order currency for.
PCMLTFA
The
Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Act
(Canada) is an act to facilitate combatting the laundering of proceeds of crime and the financing of terrorist activities.
Reference Number
The reference number is a unique number used to identify an FX Notes transaction.
Transit Number
For FX Notes purposes, the transit number is a 9-digit number that consists of a leading zero, the 3芒聙聭digit institution number followed by a 5-digit branch number (e.g., 080912010, 082812010).
User Management
The User Management application is used to add, edit, and delete Client Centre Users. It芒聙聶s also used to edit and delete Security Officers and to manage 2-step security tokens.
END
